whatThisIs: Generate a patient-authored Release of Information (ROI) request asking a covered entity to send a specified set of medical records to a named third-party recipient (another provider, the patient themselves, an attorney, an insurer, a family member). Sophie Well captures the patient and record-holder identifiers, the specific record set being requested (date range, encounter type, specialty), the recipient and delivery method (mailed paper, secure email, fax, patient portal), an expiration date, and the patient signature line, then returns a copy-paste-or-print document.
whenToUse: Use this when a patient needs records moved between two providers or sent to a non-clinical recipient and the holder is dragging or asking what they need on paper. HIPAA's individual right of access (45 CFR 164.524) gives the patient the right to obtain their own records within 30 days and is the regulatory hammer if a request stalls; most providers also have their own preferred ROI form, and many will accept this letter as a complete request or as the cover letter to their form. For records sent directly to the patient, the right-of-access pathway has stronger protections than the third-party-disclosure pathway, so when the patient is the destination, say so explicitly.
